context: Assessment conducted post-breach as part of Rockstar Games TPRM postmortem. Scores reflect inferred control state based on breach evidence.

responses.evidence: No formal credential protection policy found. Token storage allowed direct extraction by attackers.
No breach notification SLA in contract. Rockstar learned of compromise 7 days after the fact via dark web post not from Anodot.
Anodot own infrastructure was the initial attack surface indicating sub-processor security was not fully assessed.
Connector outage on April 4 was not communicated to Rockstar. No shared monitoring dashboard existed.
No cloud security requirements documented in contract covering Snowflake integration scope.
Tokens granted warehouse wide access enabling mass exfiltration of 78.6 million records.
Long lived static tokens extracted and reused by attackers. No rotation policy or expiry enforced.
Silent exfiltration went undetected for 10 days. No behavioral monitoring on query volume or off hours access.
Token extraction from Anodot systems suggests tokens were not adequately encrypted at rest.
